verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a type 1 transmembrane protein with an N-terminal extracellular domain and C-terminal cytoplasmic domain. Its primary function is intracellular — residing in the ER membrane and translocating to ER-PM junctions upon store depletion to activate SOC influx channels (via direct Orai1 gating). However, STIM1 has a documented surface pool: it cycles from the ER to the plasma membrane during store-operated calcium entry, accumulating at ER-PM junctions as part of its normal signaling mechanism. This represents dual localization with active PM trafficking. The extracellular N-terminal domain provides a topological basis for potential surface accessibility during ER-PM junctional clustering and cycling, satisfying the contextual criterion of a protein with its own membrane anchor that transiently but functionally localizes to the PM.
